endings.title: The End of an Era
endings.reason: Your body finally gives out. The empire outlives the man.

endings.title: The Dynasty That Never Was
endings.reason: The Drumpf line never takes root in America — no fortune, no Queens, no 1946 birth. History closes over the family before it ever begins. There is no Donald.

endings.title: A Life Cut Short
endings.reason: A reckless boyhood dare ends the story before it begins. The Trump name carries on without you.

endings.title: Washed Out
endings.reason: Expelled and disowned, you drift into a small, anonymous life far from any tower.

endings.title: No Empire to Inherit
endings.reason: The discrimination cases stuck and your father was indicted; the rental fortune is seized in fines and shame. With no seed capital, there is no Manhattan, no tower, no empire — just a working life and a name nobody fears.

endings.title: Quiet Succession
endings.reason: You never reached for more than enough. A comfortable old CEO of the outer-borough rentals — wealthy but not insanely so, still married to your first wife — you hand the business to your son, just as your father handed it to you. The story simply, peaceably, ends.

endings.title: Cut Down
endings.reason: A bullet finds you at the height of your notoriety. The crowd screams.

endings.title: Toppled
endings.reason: Investigations close in and your inner circle abandons you.

endings.title: Convicted
endings.reason: The cases finally stick. The cell door closes on the most famous man alive.

endings.title: Ruined
endings.reason: The debt avalanche buries you. The name comes off the buildings.

endings.title: A Quiet Life
endings.reason: You step off the stage early. History barely records your name.

endings.title: The People's Patriarch
endings.reason: You chose solidarity over self. A post-scarcity commonwealth rises in your name.

endings.title: God-Emperor
endings.reason: Unbound by law or shame, you rule as a living god. The world kneels — and seethes.

endings.title: The Tide of History
endings.reason: You did not bend the age to your will — you rode it. The world had already turned toward the commons, and you became the face it chose. A post-scarcity commonwealth, inevitable in hindsight, eternal in your name.

endings.title: Sovereign of a Willing World
endings.reason: The age itself had soured toward strongmen, and you were the strongest. No coup was needed — the world had already decided it wanted a master. You merely accepted. A living god atop a planet that asked to kneel.

endings.title: Two-World Patriarch
endings.reason: Immortal master of Earth and the first Martian city — a civilization spanning two worlds.

endings.title: Heaven on Earth
endings.reason: You never reached for the stars — but you remade the world. A serene, almost religious golden age, bounded by one blue sky.

endings.title: The Deity of a Closed World
endings.reason: No Mars, no stars — only Earth, and you above it. A deistic god-king worshipped on a planet that never looked up.

endings.title: An Earthbound Twilight
endings.reason: No Mars, no stars, no crown, no ruin — just a long American life that ran its course on one small world. History files you under the ordinary powerful: a name that mattered for a season, then a footnote. The sky stayed closed, and you never much minded.

endings.title: The Gift of the Stars
endings.reason: Because we reached out from Mars, we were seen — and the Concord judged us worthy. They gave humanity the stars themselves, at lightspeed.

endings.title: What Answered
endings.reason: Our extra-solar wake read as prey. Something far worse than us followed it home.

endings.title: Among the Stars, As Equals
endings.reason: Allied with the Concord and warp-borne, humanity spread across the light-years as a respected peer of worlds. The apex of the human story.

endings.title: Emperor of a Thousand Suns
endings.reason: You conquered what came, and turned the gift of the stars into a slaving empire. Immortal god-emperor of a darkened galaxy.

endings.title: The Long Rebellion
endings.reason: Subjugated, then unbroken — humanity threw off its overlords across a century of insurgency. Free, scarred, and finally our own.

endings.title: Administrator of a Conquered World
endings.reason: You kept your throne by handing humanity its chains — figurehead of alien masters over an occupied Earth.

endings.title: Silence
endings.reason: The rebellion failed. What answered our call finished what it came to do. The human story ends in the dark.

endings.title: The Man Behind the Throne
endings.reason: The other one took the Oval Office. You took everything else. While Musk played at empire from a desk, you built the real one — towers, rigs, rooms, the quiet machinery of money. You never needed the crown. You owned the kingdom.

endings.title: Industrialist of the Eternal Reich
endings.reason: In a world the Axis never lost, it was Musk who rose to political power and you who inherited the commercial empire that fed it. You aligned, you supplied, you prospered — a baron of an order that should never have endured. History will not be kind, but history is written by whoever you align with next.
